Reference Documents
Quality & QA/QC/QCx Levels — L1 through Warranty
OSM & Commissioning responsibilities by phase · GCUB3.1 OFE Skid Program · Rev 1.0 — May 2026

OSM — Manufacturing Group

Equipment is manufactured in-house at Spokane and Seattle locations and sent directly to site or placed into storage. OSM is responsible for the cost of parts on any defects or deficiencies found during testing across all phases.

Cx Team — Commissioning & Testing

Separate contract from manufacturing. Manages issue resolution and closure across site phases. The Cx Team is never responsible for parts costs.
Key takeaway: OSM bears full responsibility for parts costs across every phase. The Cx Team owns issue management at L2 and L3, and jointly manages with OSM during Pre-Startup and Warranty. Quality Team manages L1.
Issue Resolution Process — Project Phases
Deviation in issue resolution across phases · OSM & Cx responsibilities · Rev 1.0 — April 2026
Issue Resolution Process.docx · 818 KB
Key takeaway: The nature and ownership of issues evolves across phases. Quality Team manages L1. Cx Team owns issue management in L2 and L3. OSM and Cx Team jointly manage Pre-Startup and Warranty (L6). OSM assists throughout all phases and is responsible for the cost of all parts.
Phase Detail — click to expand
L1 Factory Testing Quality Team ▶
Executed by
401 Service Technician
Oversight: Quality Team
Scope of Issues
  • Non-conformances during factory tests
  • Component defects
  • Documentation & procedure errors
  • Pre-shipment inspection failures
  • Improper storage or packaging damage
Parts Cost
OSM — full responsibility
Resolved byQuality Team implements corrective actions, re-tests, and releases for shipment. OSM assists and covers all parts costs.
L2 Installation Verification Commissioning Team ▶
Executed by
401 Service Technician
Oversight: Commissioning Team
Scope of Issues
  • Installation mismatches
  • Connection issues identified on-site
  • Point-to-point wiring discrepancies
  • Controls issues not found in L1
  • Transit or handling damage
  • Missing parts or accessories
Parts Cost
OSM — full responsibility
Resolved byCx Team owns issue management and closure, coordinating with site and clearing for the next phase. OSM assists and covers all parts costs.
Pre-Startup OSM Issue Period On-Site Cx & OSM ▶
Executed by
401 Service Technician
Oversight: On-Site Cx & OSM
Scope of Issues
  • Issues not caught at factory or during installation
  • Transit or trade damage found on-site
  • O&M readiness gaps
  • Documentation, drawings & manuals
  • Spare parts, tools & training needs
  • Site conditions / external dependencies
Parts Cost
OSM — full responsibility
Resolved byOn-Site Commissioning and OSM jointly manage issue resolution and closure. OSM covers all parts costs.

Note: This phase captures issues arising between L2 and L3 that were not identified earlier. Because these issues originate from manufacturing, OSM bears responsibility for all associated parts costs.
L3 Startup On-Site Cx & Startup Team ▶
Executed by
401 Startup Technician
Oversight: On-Site Commissioning
Scope of Issues
  • Start-up failures
  • Control / logic issues
  • Parameter tuning required
  • Equipment interaction problems
  • Leaks identified during commissioning
  • Loose parts, fittings, or fasteners
Parts Cost
OSM — full responsibility
Resolved byCx Team owns issue management and closure until stable start-up is achieved. OSM assists and covers all parts costs.
L4/L5 Functional & Performance Testing On-Site Commissioning ▶
Executed by
401 Service Technician (support only)
Oversight: On-Site Commissioning (support only)
Scope of Issues
  • Functional failures
  • Performance not meeting specification
  • System integration issues
  • Reliability / stability problems
Parts Cost
OSM — full responsibility
Resolved byOn-Site Commissioning provides support and advisory only. OSM assists and covers all parts costs.
Warranty Warranty Period (L6) On-Site Cx & OSM ▶
Executed by
401 Service Technician
Oversight: On-Site Cx & OSM
Scope of Issues
  • Recurring failures
  • Wear and tear related issues
  • Field modifications and improvements
  • Customer-reported defects
Parts Cost
OSM — full responsibility
Resolved byOn-Site Commissioning and OSM jointly manage resolution and closure throughout the warranty period. OSM covers all parts costs.
Roles & Responsibilities Summary
PhaseExecuted ByOversight / Issue Management
L1 — Factory Testing401 Service TechnicianQuality Team
L2 — Installation Verification401 Service TechnicianCommissioning Team
Pre-Startup — OSM Issue Period401 Service TechnicianOn-Site Commissioning & OSM
L3 — Startup401 Startup TechnicianOn-Site Commissioning
L4/L5 — Functional & Performance Testing401 Service Technician (support only)On-Site Commissioning (support only)
Warranty Period (L6)401 Service TechnicianOn-Site Commissioning & OSM
Rev 1.0 · April 2026
RACI Matrix — Issue Resolution Across Project Phases
Roles and responsibilities for issue identification, resolution, and closure · Rev 1.0 — April 2026
RACI Matrix — Issue Resolution.docx · 17 KB
RACI Legend
R Responsible
A Accountable
C Consulted
I Informed
— Not involved
Activity401 Service
Technician		401 Startup
Technician		Quality
Team		Cx TeamOSMCustomerACC / GC
L1 — Factory Testing
Create factory test checklists & proceduresR—AC———
Execute factory tests & inspectionsR—AC———
Identify & log non-conformances in Cx Alloy——RC———
Implement corrective actions on defectsR—AC———
Re-test following corrective actionR—AC———
Release equipment for shipment——ACC——
Create formal Corrective Action (repetitive issues only)——AC———
Upload L1 logs into ACC (during L2)——RA—II
L2 — Installation Verification
Create L2 Installation Verification checklists———A———
Execute installation & verification activitiesR——A———
Identify & log issues in Cx Alloy———R———
Notify Quality of repetitive / duplicate issues——IR———
Create formal Corrective Action (repetitive issues only)——AR———
Perform fixes & corrective work on-siteR——A———
Validate & clear fixes———A———
Close issues in Cx Alloy (Cx Team only)———A———
Send final report to customer———ACII
Pre-Startup — Warranty Work
Log issues in Cx Alloy (internal)———RC——
Log issues in ACC platform———C——R
Respond to ACC issues (daily / every other day)———RC——
PM oversight of ACC turnaround & contractual closure———AC——
Authorize parts & labor———CA——
Execute corrective work on-siteR——CA——
Upload photo & mark issue "In-Review" in ACC———R———
Close issue in ACC———C——A
Close issue in Cx Alloy (Cx Team only)———A———
Create formal Corrective Action (repetitive issues only)——AC———
L3 — Startup
Execute startup activities—R—AC——
Log issues in Cx Alloy (internal)———RC——
Log issues in ACC platform———C——R
Troubleshoot & adjust settings—R—AC——
Perform fixes & corrective workRR—AC——
Upload photo & mark issue "In-Review" in ACC———R———
Close issue in ACC———C——A
Close issue in Cx Alloy (Cx Team only)———A———
Confirm stable start-up—R—AC——
Notify Quality of repetitive / duplicate issues——IR———
Create formal Corrective Action (repetitive issues only)——AC———
L4/L5 — Functional & Performance Testing
Execute functional & performance tests (Third Party)———AC——
Log issues in Cx Alloy (internal)———RC——
Log issues in ACC platform———C——R
Execute corrective field workR——AC——
Mobilize & oversee corrective field work———AC——
Provide advisory & technical support———AC——
Upload photo & mark issue "In-Review" in ACC———R———
Close issue in ACC———C——A
Close issue in Cx Alloy (Cx Team only)———A———
Notify customer of issue status———ACII
Create formal Corrective Action (repetitive issues only)——AC———
Warranty Period (L6)
Log issues in Cx Alloy (internal)———RC——
Log issues in ACC platform———C——R
Respond to ACC issues (daily / every other day)———RC——
Authorize parts & labor———CA——
Execute corrective field workR——CA——
Upload photo & mark issue "In-Review" in ACC———R———
Close issue in ACC———C——A
Close issue in Cx Alloy (Cx Team only)———A———
PM oversight of issue response timelines———AC——
Communicate status to customer———ACII
Create formal Corrective Action (repetitive issues only)——AC———
Key Notes
  • Issues are logged internally in Cx Alloy across all phases. Only Cx Site Team and management have privileges to close issues in Cx Alloy.
  • ACC and GC log issues in the ACC platform from Pre-Startup through Warranty (L6). The GC closes issues in ACC after the Service Technician implements the fix and the Cx Team member uploads a photo and marks the issue "In-Review".
  • Cx Team members respond to ACC issues daily or every other day. The Project Manager oversees contractual turnaround times and closure requirements.
  • L1 test logs (torque, pneumatic, etc.) are uploaded into ACC during L2, at which point the Customer and ACC/GC are informed.
  • L4/L5 testing is executed by a third-party team. 401 Service Technicians execute corrective field work when issues are identified. Cx Team oversees, mobilizes technicians, and provides advisory support throughout.
  • Customer receives a final report at L2 completion. Active communication begins from L4/L5 onward. Customer is not routinely informed of individual issue closures in earlier phases.
  • Corrective Actions are separate from basic issue resolution. Quality Team creates formal Corrective Actions only when an issue is repetitive or duplicated multiple times — applicable across all phases.
Rev 1.0 · April 2026
